# solution.py
"""TaskChain / Team Collaboration Manager.

A self-contained, thread-safe, in-memory project-management and team
collaboration engine.  It supports:

* User management (registration, lookup, update, deletion with referential
  integrity checks).
* Project management with cascading deletes.
* Task management with assignment, deadlines and lifecycle status tracking
  (start / completion timestamps).
* Messaging and collaboration (threaded messages, file attachments, editing).
* A performance dashboard (completion rate, average completion time, peer
  ratings).
* Reporting (project progress, team performance, individual performance) with
  export to CSV and PDF.

The public surface exercised by the test-suite is the
:class:`TeamCollaborationManager` class together with the supporting enums,
exception hierarchy and lightweight entity records.  ``TaskChain`` is provided
as a friendly alias for the manager.

The implementation uses standard library modules only and is written to be
ASCII-clean.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import csv
import io
import threading
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum

__all__ = [
    "TeamCollaborationManager",
    "TaskChain",
    "TaskStatus",
    "UserRole",
    "ReportFormat",
    "ReportType",
    "TeamCollaborationError",
    "ValidationError",
    "NotFoundError",
    "AuthorizationError",
    "DuplicateError",
    "ConflictError",
    "Attachment",
    "User",
    "Project",
    "Task",
    "Message",
    "Rating",
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle state of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role a user holds inside the system."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for reports."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Kinds of report the system can produce."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for every error raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input data fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an action."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an action conflicts with the current system state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Entity records
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class Attachment(object):
    """A file attached to a message."""

    def __init__(self, filename, size, uploaded_by, uploaded_at):
        self.filename = filename
        self.size = size
        self.uploaded_by = uploaded_by
        self.uploaded_at = uploaded_at

    def clone(self):
        return Attachment(
            self.filename, self.size, self.uploaded_by, self.uploaded_at
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Attachment(filename={0!r}, size={1})".format(
            self.filename, self.size
        )


class User(object):
    """A person who can own projects, be assigned tasks and collaborate."""

    def __init__(self, id, username, email, role, created_at):
        self.id = id
        self.username = username
        self.email = email
        self.role = role
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return User(
            self.id, self.username, self.email, self.role, self.created_at
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "User(id={0}, username={1!r})".format(self.id, self.username)


class Project(object):
    """A container for tasks owned by a user."""

    def __init__(self, id, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id,
                 description="", created_at=None):
        self.id = id
        self.name = name
        self.start_date = start_date
        self.end_date = end_date
        self.owner_id = owner_id
        self.description = description
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return Project(
            self.id,
            self.name,
            self.start_date,
            self.end_date,
            self.owner_id,
            self.description,
            self.created_at,
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Project(id={0}, name={1!r})".format(self.id, self.name)


class Task(object):
    """A unit of work belonging to a project."""

    def __init__(self, id, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None,
                 status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED, started_at=None,
                 completed_at=None, created_at=None):
        self.id = id
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.title = title
        self.assignee_id = assignee_id
        self.deadline = deadline
        self.status = status
        self.started_at = started_at
        self.completed_at = completed_at
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return Task(
            self.id,
            self.project_id,
            self.title,
            self.assignee_id,
            self.deadline,
            self.status,
            self.started_at,
            self.completed_at,
            self.created_at,
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Task(id={0}, title={1!r}, status={2})".format(
            self.id, self.title, self.status
        )


class Message(object):
    """A comment or note posted against a project or task."""

    def __init__(self, id, author_id, body, project_id=None, task_id=None,
                 parent_id=None, attachments=None, created_at=None,
                 edited_at=None):
        self.id = id
        self.author_id = author_id
        self.body = body
        self.project_id = project_id
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.parent_id = parent_id
        self.attachments = list(attachments) if attachments else []
        self.created_at = created_at
        self.edited_at = edited_at

    def clone(self):
        copy = Message(
            self.id,
            self.author_id,
            self.body,
            self.project_id,
            self.task_id,
            self.parent_id,
            [att.clone() for att in self.attachments],
            self.created_at,
            self.edited_at,
        )
        return copy

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Message(id={0}, author_id={1})".format(self.id, self.author_id)


class Rating(object):
    """A peer rating of one user by another."""

    def __init__(self, id, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="",
                 task_id=None, created_at=None):
        self.id = id
        self.rater_id = rater_id
        self.ratee_id = ratee_id
        self.score = score
        self.feedback = feedback
        self.task_id = task_id
        self.created_at = created_at

    def clone(self):
        return Rating(
            self.id,
            self.rater_id,
            self.ratee_id,
            self.score,
            self.feedback,
            self.task_id,
            self.created_at,
        )

    def __repr__(self):  # pragma: no cover - debugging aid
        return "Rating(id={0}, score={1})".format(self.id, self.score)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Shared data store
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _DataStore(object):
    """Holds every collection plus the global identifier counter.

    A single store instance is shared by all of the services so that they can
    enforce cross-entity invariants (referential integrity, cascading deletes,
    aggregate reporting) without duplicating data.
    """

    def __init__(self):
        self.users = {}
        self.projects = {}
        self.tasks = {}
        self.messages = {}
        self.ratings = {}
        # user_id -> set(task_id) index for fast assignee look-ups.
        self.assignee_index = {}
        self._id_counter = 0

    def next_id(self):
        """Return a process-unique, monotonically increasing identifier.

        Identifiers are global across every entity type which guarantees that
        a user, project, task and message never share an id.
        """

        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Service base class with shared helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _Service(object):
    """Common functionality for every service.

    Each service stores a reference to the shared :class:`_DataStore`, the
    shared re-entrant lock and the injectable ``now`` callable.  Because the
    lock is shared (and re-entrant) services may freely call helpers on one
    another while already holding it.
    """

    def __init__(self, state, lock, now):
        self._state = state
        self._lock = lock
        self._now = now

    # -- lookup helpers -----------------------------------------------------
    def _require_user(self, user_id):
        user = self._state.users.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("User {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id):
        project = self._state.projects.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id):
        task = self._state.tasks.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id):
        message = self._state.messages.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("Message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    # -- validation helpers -------------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _clean_text(value, field):
        if not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip():
            raise ValidationError(
                "{0} must be a non-empty string".format(field)
            )
        return value.strip()

    @staticmethod
    def _clean_email(value):
        if not isinstance(value, str):
            raise ValidationError("email must be a string")
        email = value.strip()
        if not email or "@" not in email:
            raise ValidationError("email must be a valid address")
        return email

    # -- assignee index helpers --------------------------------------------
    def _set_assignee(self, task, new_assignee_id):
        """Move ``task`` to a new assignee, keeping the index consistent."""

        old = task.assignee_id
        if old is not None:
            bucket = self._state.assignee_index.get(old)
            if bucket is not None:
                bucket.discard(task.id)
                if not bucket:
                    del self._state.assignee_index[old]
        task.assignee_id = new_assignee_id
        if new_assignee_id is not None:
            self._state.assignee_index.setdefault(
                new_assignee_id, set()
            ).add(task.id)

    def _tasks_for_assignee(self, user_id):
        ids = self._state.assignee_index.get(user_id, set())
        return [
            self._state.tasks[tid]
            for tid in ids
            if tid in self._state.tasks
        ]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# User service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserService(_Service):
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        with self._lock:
            username = self._clean_text(username, "username")
            email = self._clean_email(email)
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            self._check_unique(username, email, exclude_id=None)
            uid = self._state.next_id()
            user = User(uid, username, email, role, self._now())
            self._state.users[uid] = user
            return user.clone()

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_user(user_id).clone()

    def list_users(self):
        with self._lock:
            users = [u.clone() for u in self._state.users.values()]
            users.sort(key=lambda u: u.id)
            return users

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)
            new_username = user.username
            new_email = user.email
            new_role = user.role
            if username is not None:
                new_username = self._clean_text(username, "username")
            if email is not None:
                new_email = self._clean_email(email)
            if role is not None:
                if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                    raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
                new_role = role
            self._check_unique(new_username, new_email, exclude_id=user_id)
            user.username = new_username
            user.email = new_email
            user.role = new_role
            return user.clone()

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            for project in self._state.projects.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "User {0} owns project {1}".format(
                            user_id, project.id
                        )
                    )
            for task in self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id):
                if task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError(
                        "User {0} still has open task {1}".format(
                            user_id, task.id
                        )
                    )
            self._state.assignee_index.pop(user_id, None)
            del self._state.users[user_id]

    def _check_unique(self, username, email, exclude_id):
        for other in self._state.users.values():
            if other.id == exclude_id:
                continue
            if other.username == username:
                raise DuplicateError(
                    "username {0!r} already in use".format(username)
                )
            if other.email == email:
                raise DuplicateError(
                    "email {0!r} already in use".format(email)
                )


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Project service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ProjectService(_Service):
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id,
                       description=""):
        with self._lock:
            name = self._clean_text(name, "name")
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")
            if description is None:
                description = ""
            pid = self._state.next_id()
            project = Project(
                pid, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._state.projects[pid] = project
            return project.clone()

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_project(project_id).clone()

    def list_projects(self):
        with self._lock:
            projects = [p.clone() for p in self._state.projects.values()]
            projects.sort(key=lambda p: p.id)
            return projects

    def update_project(self, project_id, name=None, start_date=None,
                       end_date=None, description=None):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            new_name = project.name
            new_start = project.start_date
            new_end = project.end_date
            new_desc = project.description
            if name is not None:
                new_name = self._clean_text(name, "name")
            if start_date is not None:
                new_start = start_date
            if end_date is not None:
                new_end = end_date
            if description is not None:
                new_desc = description
            if new_end < new_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date must not precede start_date")
            project.name = new_name
            project.start_date = new_start
            project.end_date = new_end
            project.description = new_desc
            return project.clone()

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            doomed_tasks = [
                t.id
                for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            doomed_task_set = set(doomed_tasks)
            for task_id in doomed_tasks:
                task = self._state.tasks.pop(task_id)
                self._set_assignee(task, None)
            doomed_messages = [
                m.id
                for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id or m.task_id in doomed_task_set
            ]
            for message_id in doomed_messages:
                del self._state.messages[message_id]
            del self._state.projects[project_id]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Task service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TaskService(_Service):
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None):
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            title = self._clean_text(title, "title")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "deadline must not precede the project start date"
                )
            tid = self._state.next_id()
            task = Task(
                tid,
                project_id,
                title,
                assignee_id=None,
                deadline=deadline,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self._state.tasks[tid] = task
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return task.clone()

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            return self._require_task(task_id).clone()

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                t.clone()
                for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return tasks

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            tasks = [t.clone() for t in self._tasks_for_assignee(assignee_id)]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return tasks

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return task.clone()

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, assignee_id)
            return task.clone()

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._set_assignee(task, None)
            return task.clone()

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._state.projects.get(task.project_id)
                if project is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError(
                        "deadline must not precede the project start date"
                    )
            task.deadline = deadline
            return task.clone()

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            now = self._now()
            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            else:  # NOT_STARTED
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None
            task.status = status
            return task.clone()


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Message service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class MessageService(_Service):
    def post_message(self, author_id, body, project_id=None, task_id=None,
                     parent_id=None, attachments=None):
        with self._lock:
            targets = [t for t in (project_id, task_id) if t is not None]
            if len(targets) != 1:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            self._require_user(author_id)
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            else:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)
            attachment_objs = self._build_attachments(attachments, author_id)
            if (body is None or not str(body).strip()) and not attachment_objs:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "message must have a body or at least one attachment"
                )
            body = body if body is not None else ""
            mid = self._state.next_id()
            message = Message(
                mid,
                author_id,
                body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                attachments=attachment_objs,
                created_at=self._now(),
                edited_at=None,
            )
            self._state.messages[mid] = message
            return message.clone()

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != editor_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may edit this message"
                )
            has_body = new_body is not None and str(new_body).strip()
            if not has_body and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError(
                    "message must have a body or at least one attachment"
                )
            message.body = new_body if new_body is not None else ""
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return message.clone()

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, uploader_id, filename, data):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != uploader_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may attach files to this message"
                )
            filename = self._clean_text(filename, "filename")
            attachment = Attachment(
                filename, len(data), uploader_id, self._now()
            )
            message.attachments.append(attachment)
            return message.clone()

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            messages = [
                m.clone()
                for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.project_id == project_id
            ]
            messages.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return messages

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            messages = [
                m.clone()
                for m in self._state.messages.values()
                if m.task_id == task_id
            ]
            messages.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return messages

    def delete_message(self, message_id, requester_id):
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != requester_id:
                raise AuthorizationError(
                    "only the author may delete this message"
                )
            del self._state.messages[message_id]

    def _build_attachments(self, attachments, author_id):
        result = []
        if not attachments:
            return result
        now = self._now()
        for entry in attachments:
            filename, data = entry
            filename = self._clean_text(filename, "filename")
            result.append(Attachment(filename, len(data), author_id, now))
        return result


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Performance service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class PerformanceService(_Service):
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            if not tasks:
                return 0.0
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            return completed / float(len(tasks))

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            durations = self._completion_durations(
                self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            )
            if not durations:
                return None
            return sum(durations) / float(len(durations))

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="",
                      task_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("users may not rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer 1..5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)
            rid = self._state.next_id()
            rating = Rating(
                rid,
                rater_id,
                ratee_id,
                score,
                feedback if feedback is not None else "",
                task_id,
                self._now(),
            )
            self._state.ratings[rid] = rating
            return rating.clone()

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = [
                r
                for r in self._state.ratings.values()
                if r.ratee_id == user_id
            ]
            ratings.sort(key=lambda r: r.id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / float(count)
                if count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [r.clone() for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            total = len(tasks)
            completed = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            )
            in_progress = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
            )
            not_started = sum(
                1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
            )
            durations = self._completion_durations(tasks)
            average_time = (
                sum(durations) / float(len(durations)) if durations else None
            )
            summary = self.get_ratings_summary(user_id)
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "in_progress_tasks": in_progress,
                "not_started_tasks": not_started,
                "completion_rate": (
                    completed / float(total) if total else 0.0
                ),
                "average_completion_time": average_time,
                "rating_count": summary["count"],
                "rating_average": summary["average"],
            }

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_durations(tasks):
        durations = []
        for task in tasks:
            if (
                task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                and task.started_at is not None
                and task.completed_at is not None
            ):
                delta = task.completed_at - task.started_at
                durations.append(delta.total_seconds())
        return durations


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Report service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class ReportService(_Service):
    def __init__(self, state, lock, now, performance):
        super(ReportService, self).__init__(state, lock, now)
        self._performance = performance

    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            tasks = [
                t
                for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                if t.project_id == project_id
            ]
            total = len(tasks)
            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            completed = 0
            overdue = 0
            now = self._now()
            per_assignee = {}
            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                if (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                    and task.deadline < now
                ):
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id,
                        {
                            "total_tasks": 0,
                            "completed_tasks": 0,
                            "in_progress_tasks": 0,
                            "not_started_tasks": 0,
                        },
                    )
                    bucket["total_tasks"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed_tasks"] += 1
                    elif task.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                        bucket["in_progress_tasks"] += 1
                    else:
                        bucket["not_started_tasks"] += 1
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completed_tasks": completed,
                "completion_rate": (
                    completed / float(total) if total else 0.0
                ),
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scoped_tasks = [
                    t
                    for t in self._state.tasks.values()
                    if t.project_id == project_id
                ]
            else:
                scoped_tasks = list(self._state.tasks.values())
            by_user = {}
            for task in scoped_tasks:
                if task.assignee_id is None:
                    continue
                by_user.setdefault(task.assignee_id, []).append(task)
            rows = []
            for user_id in sorted(by_user):
                tasks = by_user[user_id]
                rows.append(self._performance_row(user_id, tasks))
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._tasks_for_assignee(user_id)
            tasks = sorted(tasks, key=lambda t: t.id)
            row = self._performance_row(user_id, tasks)
            task_views = [
                {
                    "id": t.id,
                    "project_id": t.project_id,
                    "title": t.title,
                    "status": t.status.value,
                    "deadline": t.deadline,
                    "started_at": t.started_at,
                    "completed_at": t.completed_at,
                }
                for t in tasks
            ]
            report = {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": row["total_tasks"],
                "completed_tasks": row["completed_tasks"],
                "completion_rate": row["completion_rate"],
                "average_completion_time": row["average_completion_time"],
                "rating_count": row["rating_count"],
                "rating_average": row["rating_average"],
                "tasks": task_views,
            }
            return report

    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("report must be a mapping")
            if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")
            if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return _report_to_csv(report)
            return _report_to_pdf(report)

    def _performance_row(self, user_id, tasks):
        total = len(tasks)
        completed = sum(
            1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
        )
        durations = PerformanceService._completion_durations(tasks)
        average_time = (
            sum(durations) / float(len(durations)) if durations else None
        )
        summary = self._performance.get_ratings_summary(user_id)
        user = self._state.users.get(user_id)
        return {
            "user_id": user_id,
            "username": user.username if user is not None else "",
            "total_tasks": total,
            "completed_tasks": completed,
            "completion_rate": completed / float(total) if total else 0.0,
            "average_completion_time": average_time,
            "rating_count": summary["count"],
            "rating_average": summary["average"],
        }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Export helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _stringify(value):
    """Render a report value as a flat, CSV/PDF-friendly string."""

    if value is None:
        return ""
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return "true" if value else "false"
    if isinstance(value, Enum):
        return str(value.value)
    if isinstance(value, datetime):
        return value.isoformat()
    if isinstance(value, float):
        return repr(value)
    if isinstance(value, dict):
        return "; ".join(
            "{0}={1}".format(k, _stringify(v)) for k, v in value.items()
        )
    if isinstance(value, (list, tuple)):
        return "; ".join(_stringify(v) for v in value)
    return str(value)


def _report_to_csv(report):
    """Serialise a report dict into CSV text.

    Reports that expose a list of homogeneous ``rows`` are rendered as a table;
    anything else is rendered as a two-column ``field,value`` listing.  Either
    way the first emitted row acts as a header so the output is always
    parseable by :func:`csv.reader`.
    """

    output = io.StringIO()
    writer = csv.writer(output)
    rows = report.get("rows")
    if (
        isinstance(rows, list)
        and rows
        and all(isinstance(row, dict) for row in rows)
    ):
        header = list(rows[0].keys())
        writer.writerow(header)
        for row in rows:
            writer.writerow([_stringify(row.get(col)) for col in header])
    else:
        writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "rows":
                continue
            writer.writerow([key, _stringify(value)])
    return output.getvalue()


def _report_to_lines(report):
    lines = []
    rows = report.get("rows")
    if (
        isinstance(rows, list)
        and rows
        and all(isinstance(row, dict) for row in rows)
    ):
        header = list(rows[0].keys())
        lines.append(" | ".join(str(col) for col in header))
        for row in rows:
            lines.append(
                " | ".join(_stringify(row.get(col)) for col in header)
            )
    else:
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "rows":
                continue
            lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, _stringify(value)))
    return lines


def _escape_pdf_text(text):
    return (
        text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
        .replace("(", "\\(")
        .replace(")", "\\)")
    )


def _report_to_pdf(report):
    """Build a minimal but well-formed single-page PDF document.

    The document embeds a textual rendering of the report.  Cross-reference
    offsets are computed precisely so the resulting bytes are a valid PDF that
    begins with the ``%PDF-`` signature.
    """

    title = "Report: {0}".format(
        _stringify(report.get("report_type", "report"))
    )
    body_lines = _report_to_lines(report)

    content_parts = ["BT", "/F1 12 Tf", "72 760 Td", "14 TL"]
    content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(_escape_pdf_text(title)))
    for line in body_lines:
        content_parts.append("T*")
        content_parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(_escape_pdf_text(line)))
    content_parts.append("ET")
    stream = "\n".join(content_parts).encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
        (
            b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
            b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 5 0 R >> >> /Contents 4 0 R >>"
        ),
        (
            b"<< /Length "
            + str(len(stream)).encode("ascii")
            + b" >>\nstream\n"
            + stream
            + b"\nendstream"
        ),
        b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
    ]

    out = bytearray()
    out += b"%PDF-1.4\n"
    offsets = []
    for index, obj in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(out))
        out += str(index).encode("ascii") + b" 0 obj\n"
        out += obj
        out += b"\nendobj\n"

    xref_pos = len(out)
    size = len(objects) + 1
    out += b"xref\n"
    out += b"0 " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
    out += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        out += ("%010d 00000 n \n" % offset).encode("ascii")
    out += b"trailer\n"
    out += b"<< /Size " + str(size).encode("ascii") + b" /Root 1 0 R >>\n"
    out += b"startxref\n"
    out += str(xref_pos).encode("ascii") + b"\n"
    out += b"%%EOF\n"
    return bytes(out)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Facade manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager(object):
    """Top-level facade tying every service together.

    All services share a single re-entrant lock and a single data store which
    keeps the whole system thread-safe and internally consistent.  A ``now``
    callable may be injected for deterministic testing; it defaults to
    :func:`datetime.datetime.now`.
    """

    def __init__(self, now=None):
        self._now = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._state = _DataStore()

        self.users = UserService(self._state, self._lock, self._now)
        self.projects = ProjectService(self._state, self._lock, self._now)
        self.tasks = TaskService(self._state, self._lock, self._now)
        self.messages = MessageService(self._state, self._lock, self._now)
        self.performance = PerformanceService(
            self._state, self._lock, self._now
        )
        self.reports = ReportService(
            self._state, self._lock, self._now, self.performance
        )

    # -- user management ----------------------------------------------------
    def register_user(self, username, email, role=UserRole.MEMBER):
        return self.users.register_user(username, email, role)

    def get_user(self, user_id):
        return self.users.get_user(user_id)

    def list_users(self):
        return self.users.list_users()

    def update_user(self, user_id, username=None, email=None, role=None):
        return self.users.update_user(
            user_id, username=username, email=email, role=role
        )

    def delete_user(self, user_id):
        return self.users.delete_user(user_id)

    # -- project management -------------------------------------------------
    def create_project(self, name, start_date, end_date, owner_id,
                       description=""):
        return self.projects.create_project(
            name, start_date, end_date, owner_id, description
        )

    def get_project(self, project_id):
        return self.projects.get_project(project_id)

    def list_projects(self):
        return self.projects.list_projects()

    def update_project(self, project_id, name=None, start_date=None,
                       end_date=None, description=None):
        return self.projects.update_project(
            project_id,
            name=name,
            start_date=start_date,
            end_date=end_date,
            description=description,
        )

    def delete_project(self, project_id):
        return self.projects.delete_project(project_id)

    # -- task management ----------------------------------------------------
    def create_task(self, project_id, title, assignee_id=None, deadline=None):
        return self.tasks.create_task(
            project_id, title, assignee_id=assignee_id, deadline=deadline
        )

    def get_task(self, task_id):
        return self.tasks.get_task(task_id)

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id):
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_project(project_id)

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.list_tasks_by_assignee(assignee_id)

    def assign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id, assignee_id):
        return self.tasks.reassign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id):
        return self.tasks.unassign_task(task_id)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id, deadline):
        return self.tasks.set_deadline(task_id, deadline)

    def update_status(self, task_id, status):
        return self.tasks.update_status(task_id, status)

    # -- messaging ----------------------------------------------------------
    def post_message(self, author_id, body, project_id=None, task_id=None,
                     parent_id=None, attachments=None):
        return self.messages.post_message(
            author_id,
            body,
            project_id=project_id,
            task_id=task_id,
            parent_id=parent_id,
            attachments=attachments,
        )

    def edit_message(self, message_id, editor_id, new_body):
        return self.messages.edit_message(message_id, editor_id, new_body)

    def add_attachment(self, message_id, uploader_id, filename, data):
        return self.messages.add_attachment(
            message_id, uploader_id, filename, data
        )

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id):
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_project(project_id)

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id):
        return self.messages.list_messages_by_task(task_id)

    def delete_message(self, message_id, requester_id):
        return self.messages.delete_message(message_id, requester_id)

    # -- performance --------------------------------------------------------
    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_completion_rate(user_id)

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_average_completion_time(user_id)

    def submit_rating(self, rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback="",
                      task_id=None):
        return self.performance.submit_rating(
            rater_id, ratee_id, score, feedback=feedback, task_id=task_id
        )

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_ratings_summary(user_id)

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id):
        return self.performance.get_user_dashboard(user_id)

    # -- reporting ----------------------------------------------------------
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id):
        return self.reports.project_progress_report(project_id)

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id=None):
        return self.reports.team_performance_report(project_id)

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id):
        return self.reports.individual_performance_report(user_id)

    def export_report(self, report, report_format):
        return self.reports.export_report(report, report_format)


# Friendly alias matching the product name from the original brief.
TaskChain = TeamCollaborationManager


def _demo():  # pragma: no cover - illustrative usage only
    """A tiny end-to-end demonstration of the public API."""

    manager = TeamCollaborationManager()
    owner = manager.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    worker = manager.register_user("bob", "bob@example.com")
    project = manager.create_project(
        "Launch",
        datetime.now(),
        datetime.now().replace(year=datetime.now().year + 1),
        owner.id,
        "Ship the product",
    )
    design = manager.create_task(
        project.id, "Design", assignee_id=worker.id
    )
    manager.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    manager.update_status(design.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    manager.post_message(worker.id, "Design is done!", task_id=design.id)
    report = manager.project_progress_report(project.id)
    print("Project progress report:")
    print(_report_to_csv(report))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
